highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.170: Claude Fable 5 Now Available
highlights.summary: Anthropic has released Claude Fable 5, a Mythos-class model described as exceeding the capabilities of any model previously made generally available. Update to Claude Code v2.1.170 to gain access to Fable 5 immediately. This is a significant upgrade for developers looking for more powerful AI assistance in their coding workflows.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.169: Safe Mode Flag and /cd Command Added
highlights.summary: The new `--safe-mode` flag (or `CLAUDE_CODE_SAFE_MODE` env variable) launches Claude Code with all customizations disabled — including CLAUDE.md, plugins, hooks, and MCP servers — making it much easier to troubleshoot unexpected behavior. A new `/cd` command lets you switch your session's working directory on the fly without losing your prompt cache, saving time when navigating multi-directory projects.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.166: Fallback Models and Glob Deny Rules
highlights.summary: You can now configure up to three fallback models in the `fallbackModel` setting, so Claude Code automatically switches to an alternative when your primary model is overloaded or unavailable — keeping your workflow uninterrupted. Glob pattern support in deny rules (e.g. `"*"` to deny all tools) gives you much finer-grained control over what Claude Code is allowed to do in your projects.

highlights.title: Show HN: Rayline Routes Claude Code Sub-agents to Cheaper or On-Device Models
highlights.summary: Rayline is a Claude Code-compatible LLM gateway that intercepts Claude Code's internal routing, letting you run your main agent on a powerful model like Opus while routing sub-agent calls to cheaper cloud-hosted or even on-device models. This can dramatically cut API costs for large agentic workflows without sacrificing quality on the tasks that matter most. Worth evaluating if you run multi-step or swarm-style Claude Code sessions regularly.

highlights.title: Show HN: Lessons Learned from Running Claude Code Swarms at Scale
highlights.summary: A developer shares hard-won lessons from building and running 'fleet', a Python orchestrator for coordinating swarms of Claude Code agents in parallel. The post covers practical challenges like state management, coordination patterns, and cost control when scaling agentic workflows beyond a single session. Essential reading for anyone exploring multi-agent Claude Code setups.

highlights.title: Headroom: Doubles Claude Code Usage by Trimming Prompt Bloat
highlights.summary: Headroom is a macOS menu bar app that silently optimizes the prompts Claude Code sends by stripping boilerplate, trimming excess context, and compressing documents — without changing your workflow at all. The result is roughly 2x more effective usage out of your existing Claude subscription by reducing token consumption. A practical, low-effort win for developers who hit usage limits frequently.

highlights.title: Ultimate Claude Code Guide: How to Use Claude Code for Beginners in 2026
highlights.summary: A comprehensive beginner-focused YouTube tutorial walking through how to get started with Claude Code in 2026, covering installation, basic commands, and practical use cases. If you're new to Claude Code, this is a solid starting point to understand the core workflow before diving into more advanced features. Pair it with the official docs for the best onboarding experience.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube video offers a focused 30-minute deep-dive into Claude Code for developers who already know the basics and want to level up their skills quickly. It covers more advanced workflows and productivity techniques in a compact, time-efficient format. A great watch for intermediate users looking to get more out of Claude Code without committing to a multi-hour course.

tips_of_the_day: If Claude Code is behaving unexpectedly, run it with `--safe-mode` to disable all CLAUDE.md files, hooks, and MCP servers at once — this instantly rules out customization conflicts and helps you isolate the root cause of any issue.

Set up a `fallbackModel` in your Claude Code settings with at least one backup model. This means high-traffic periods or model outages won't block your coding session — Claude Code will silently retry with the next model in your list.

Before your next big agentic session, use the `/cd` command to organize your work: start in a high-level project directory, then use `/cd` to move into specific sub-folders as tasks evolve — you'll keep your prompt cache intact and avoid reloading context from scratch.
